reasoning: While programmatic signals show high content preservation (97.5% recall), the structural and visual fidelity scores are poor (0.72 and 0.50) due to a collapse of the document's primary layout. The conversion correctly identified the tabular agenda structure for the first five items but abandoned it for the remaining eight, resulting in a loss of semantic relationship between topics and presenters, and flattening financial data into unreadable text blocks.

concerns.analysis: The source document presents the entire agenda in a consistent three-column layout (Tab, Topic, Presenter). The output correctly identifies this as a table for the first eight items (header through item 5). However, the table structure is abandoned after item 5. Agenda items 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, and 13 are converted as plain text paragraphs instead of continuing the table structure. This results in the table containing only 8 data rows compared to the 16 visible in the source's structured lis
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source document list from item 6 (Executive Director Report) through item 13 (Other) on pages 1 and 2 follows the Tab/Topic/Presenter columnar format.
concerns.output_quote: The 'rows' array in the 'table' element (index 3) ends after item 5 (Consent Agenda). Subsequent agenda items are placed in paragraph elements 5 and 6.

concerns.analysis: The source document uses a table-like structure for the entire agenda (items 1-13) with distinct columns for 'Tab', 'Topic', and 'Presenter'. The output correctly identifies this structure for items 1-5, but then terminates the table. Items 6 through 13 are rendered as flat paragraphs, which corrupts the structural relationship between the topics and the presenters (e.g., in the output, 'Pokey Harris' is merely appended to the end of the text for item 6 instead of being in its own cell).
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Item 6 (Executive Director Report) through Item 13 (Other) are aligned with the 'Topic' and 'Presenter' column headers.
concerns.output_quote: The table element ends after item 5. Item 6 is in a paragraph: '6. Executive Director Report…………………………………………………..………Pokey Harris'. Items 7-13 are merged into a single paragraph at the end of the document.
concerns.location: Agenda table and subsequent paragraphs, Page 1 and Page 2

concerns.analysis: The source PDF presents the entire agenda (items 1 through 13) in a consistent three-column layout (Tab, Topic, Presenter). In the HTML output, this structure is broken: only items 1 through 5 are contained within a table, while items 6 through 13 are rendered as dense text paragraphs. For these latter items, the 'Presenter' names are simply appended to the end of the line, losing the clear vertical alignment and scanning capability provided by the source document's layout.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source PDF on pages 1 and 2 shows items 1-13 organized in columns with presenters right-aligned.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML table ends at item 5. Item 6 and items 7-13 are rendered as text blocks below the table.

concerns.analysis: In the source PDF, Item 5 (Consent Agenda) sub-items 'b' through 'e' contain financial figures organized into clear columns for 'Month-End Balance' and 'Disbursement'. The HTML output fails to maintain this structure, flattening the data into a single, unformatted paragraph where the dollar amounts are part of a continuous string of text. This loss of alignment makes the financial data significantly harder to read and understand.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Item 5 on page 1 shows sub-items b, c, d, and e with two columns of aligned dollar amounts.
concerns.output_quote: "b) NG 911 Fund/Account April 2020 Month-End Balance $ 66,897,707 April 2020 Disbursement $ 65,036"

concerns.analysis: The source contains complex nested lists (e.g., item 5 contains sub-items a-f, and 5f contains i-ii). The output fails to preserve this structure, instead merging entire sub-lists into single text blocks within paragraphs. No list-type elements are used in the JSON output to represent these relationships.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: 5. Consent Agenda... a) Minutes of Previous Meetings... f) Grant Project Updates... i. Monthly Reports per PSAP
concerns.output_quote: A single paragraph following the table contains all text from 'a)' through 'ii.' concatenated together.
concerns.location: Consent Agenda and Executive Director Report sections, Page 1

action_items: Restore the three-column table structure for the entire agenda (items 1-13).
Properly format the financial figures in item 5 to preserve columnar alignment.
Implement correct HTML list tags for sub-items a-f and i-ii.
